executive_summary.overview: UPS engaged Anglepoint for comprehensive IBM license management covering up to 250 IBM products across 70,000 servers, 270,000 workstations, 5 mainframes, and 10,000 Red Hat servers. The engagement identified $8.9M in total optimization opportunities through risk mitigation and license right-sizing across both sub-capacity and non-ILMT products.
key_accomplishments: Achieved full $1.08M cost avoidance by resolving compliance risks across 8 IBM products including InfoSphere DataStage, WebSphere Application Server, and DB2 editions
Identified $3.59M in potential cost savings from sub-capacity products with 23 products having more licenses than deployed
Identified $2.56M in potential cost savings from non-ILMT products with 43 products over-licensed
Reduced IBM ODM Server deployment from 23,400 to 7,420 licenses representing $858K in deployment trend savings
Completed Effective License Position analysis across 115 total IBM distributed products

key_metrics.label: Total Optimization Identified
key_metrics.context: Combined cost avoidance and savings opportunities over 1-year period

key_metrics.label: Sub-Capacity Products Over-Licensed
key_metrics.context: Products with more licenses than current deployment requires

key_metrics.label: Non-ILMT Products Over-Licensed
key_metrics.context: Products with excess licenses identified for potential renewal reduction

key_metrics.label: Zero-Deployment Products (Sub-Capacity)
key_metrics.context: Products carrying active S&S with no deployments detected

key_metrics.label: Zero-Deployment Products (Non-ILMT)
key_metrics.context: Non-ILMT products with active maintenance but no usage

recommendations: Remove 23 IBM sub-capacity products with excess licenses from upcoming renewal if no growth planned - $2.34M potential savings
Validate and remove 17 sub-capacity products with 0% deployment from renewal - $2.91M potential savings
Remove 43 non-ILMT products with excess licenses from upcoming renewal - $1.82M potential savings
Confirm 32 non-ILMT products with 0% deployment are not in use and remove from renewal - $743K potential savings
Schedule WatsonX implementation with product stakeholders as next phase initiative
primary_risks: IBM DB2 Enterprise Server Edition deployment increased from 43,280 to 46,800 representing $107K in additional licensing exposure
IBM InfoSphere DataStage for Non-Production increased from 10,220 to 11,060 adding $12.5K in deployment costs
ELA renewal deadline of June 30, 2025 requires immediate action on license optimization decisions
IASP ELP delivery to IBM due July 31, 2025 for all sub-capacity products under IASP agreement
market_risks: IBM sub-capacity licensing requires continuous ILMT monitoring to maintain compliance position
Products with 0% utilization need deployment validation to confirm actual usage versus discovery gaps
IBM Cloud Pak and containerized product licensing adds complexity to consumption tracking
additional_insights: IBM Cloud Pak for Business Automation at 36.59% utilization represents $768K optimization opportunity
IBM Storage Suite for IBM Cloud Paks shows 0% utilization on 200 licenses worth $592K
IBM InfoSphere Master Data Management Family at 24.11% utilization offers $807K savings potential
Deployment trends show positive reduction in 10 products including $85K savings from IBM MQ reduction
next_steps: Implement cost savings recommendations by June 30, 2025 target date
Schedule WatsonX implementation with product stakeholders
Begin Non-PVU data collection for annual baseline establishment
Determine license counts for ELA renewal due June 30, 2025
Submit IASP ELP to IBM by July 31, 2025 with Anglepoint copied

executive_summary.overview: Regeneron engaged Anglepoint for a Quest software Risk & Opportunity Assessment Report (ROAR) with a renewal date of September 15th, 2025. The engagement identified $165K in total compliance risk and $24K in cost avoidance opportunities, with $141K of initial risk already mitigated through additional license purchases.
key_accomplishments: Identified and quantified $165K in total compliance risk exposure across Quest software estate
Achieved $141K risk mitigation through procurement of additional licenses for Change Auditor for AD and Recovery Manager for AD
Identified $24K in actionable cost avoidance opportunities
Completed comprehensive Effective License Position analysis across Quest product portfolio

key_metrics.label: Total Annual Quest Spend
key_metrics.context: Based on 2024-2025 annual spend for Quest software estate

key_metrics.label: Total Identified Risk
key_metrics.context: Quantified financial exposure due to non-compliance with Quest licensing terms

key_metrics.label: Identified Cost Avoidance
key_metrics.context: Unbudgeted costs that can be prevented through proactive compliance measures

key_metrics.label: Risk Mitigated
key_metrics.context: Initial risk reduced through additional license purchases in 2025 renewal

recommendations: Obtain Toad License Key Information: Request screenshots from end users to determine the edition of Toad deployed to improve Consumption Analysis accuracy
Optimize Toad Installations: Remove all installations and traces of Toad products (including components) that are not in use from the environment
Prioritize licensing decisions based on what is installed, licensed, and needed framework to optimize spend
primary_risks: Remaining compliance risk exposure after mitigation requires continued monitoring ahead of September 15th, 2025 renewal
Incomplete Toad license key information may result in inaccurate consumption analysis and unidentified compliance gaps
Software deployed but not needed represents ongoing cost exposure and compliance risk
market_risks: Quest renewal approaching September 15th, 2025 requires proactive preparation to optimize contract terms
Script output completeness gaps may impact accuracy of license position analysis
additional_insights: Year-over-Year analysis demonstrates effective risk mitigation through strategic license purchases
3-18 month demand forecasting data collection underway to support proactive license planning
No contracts were provided as part of this review, limiting contract terms analysis
next_steps: Procure Additional Licenses - COMPLETED: Additional licenses for Change Auditor for AD and Recovery Manager for AD purchased
Obtain Toad License Key Information: Request end user screenshots to determine deployed Toad editions
Optimize Installations: Remove unused Toad products and components from the environment

executive_summary.overview: Encova Insurance engaged Anglepoint for IBM software asset management services, covering 9 distributed IBM products across 262 servers and 50 containers with 1 ILMT instance. This ROAR documents the baseline analysis identifying compliance risks and cost optimization opportunities for their IBM estate.
key_accomplishments: Identified and accomplished $222K in cost avoidance through proactive compliance and financial controls
Achieved $282K in accomplished cost optimization from the $507K total identified optimization potential
Completed comprehensive analysis of 262 servers and 50 containers running IBM software
Established baseline Effective License Position for 9 distributed IBM products

key_metrics.label: Identified Risk
key_metrics.context: Financial exposure due to non-compliance with IBM licensing terms

key_metrics.label: Accomplished Cost Avoidance
key_metrics.context: Prevented unbudgeted costs through proactive compliance measures

key_metrics.label: Total Identified Optimization
key_metrics.context: Potential cost avoidance and savings over 1-year period

key_metrics.label: Accomplished Cost Optimization
key_metrics.context: Verified cost reductions achieved through license optimization

key_metrics.label: Servers Analyzed
key_metrics.context: Servers running IBM software included in scope

key_metrics.label: Containers Analyzed
key_metrics.context: Containers running IBM software included in scope

recommendations: Continue IBM CVA Services to maintain ongoing IBM software license management through the CVA program
Execute Annual S&S Renewal considering potential reduction opportunities identified in analysis
Expand publisher scope to include Oracle, Microsoft, VMware and other major publishers for comprehensive SAM coverage
primary_risks: Identified $222K financial exposure due to non-compliance with IBM software licensing or contractual terms requiring corrective action
market_risks: IBM licensing complexity across distributed products creates ongoing compliance risk without continuous management
additional_insights: Engagement covered 9 distributed IBM products with deployment discovery completed across infrastructure
$225K optimization opportunity gap remains between identified ($507K) and accomplished ($282K) cost optimization
ILMT instance provides foundation for ongoing PVU license tracking and compliance monitoring
next_steps: Continue quarterly CVA submission for ongoing IBM license management
Execute SOW renewal to maintain managed services continuity
Complete Annual S&S Renewal evaluation for potential cost reduction opportunities
Evaluate expansion to additional publishers including Oracle, Microsoft, and VMware
